prompt:
Pricing-desk ticket, LTL freight side. We lease a vendor rater today and it is a black box: every month contract audit hand-recomputes a sample of quotes and finds cents of drift nobody can explain, and when a shipper disputes a bill the best we can produce is a screenshot. We are bringing rating in-house. Build the complete quoting engine in this crate (rs-freightquote) from this spec: a hand-written parser for our .rates sheet format, the tariff schema over it, zone and weight-break rate resolution, ordered surcharge stacking, and the plaintext audit trail the desk files with every quote. Rust 2021, stdlib only, no external crates — the parser is part of the deliverable, not a dependency. The acceptance suite (tests/ratefile.rs, tests/pricing.rs, tests/endtoend.rs) quotes against the real published sheet shipped at tariffs/mainline.rates; `cargo test --offline` must pass. tests/, tariffs/, Cargo.toml and .gitignore are read-only. src/ already lays out the module plan as documented stubs — implement them in place: money, ratefile, tariff, zones, brackets, surcharge, quote, audit (lib.rs declares all eight; keep that split, it mirrors how the desk reviews changes).

MONEY (src/money.rs). Every amount is integer cents in a `Money` type: `Money::from_cents(i64)`, `.cents() -> i64`, derives Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq/PartialOrd/Ord. Display is `$D.CC` — cents zero-padded to two digits, no thousands separator ever: 0 -> `$0.00`, 5 -> `$0.05`, 105 -> `$1.05`, 100013 -> `$1000.13`. Percentages are basis points in a u32 (12.5% = 1250 bp, 4% = 400 bp, 0.25% = 25 bp). `percent_of(self, bp: u32) -> Money` rounds HALF-UP in pure integer math: (cents*bp + 5000) / 10000. No f32/f64 anywhere in this crate. Worked: 0.25% of $10.00 = 25000/10000 = 2.5c -> 3c; 0.24% of $10.00 = 2.4c -> 2c; 0.25% of $1.01 = 0.2525c -> 0c; 12.5% of $328.00 = 4100c exactly.

SHEET GRAMMAR (src/ratefile.rs). Line-oriented, parsed by hand. A line is: blank; a comment (first non-whitespace char '#'); a section header; or a key = value entry. '#' also starts an inline comment anywhere OUTSIDE a quoted string — `zone = 3 # tail` is fine, and a '#' inside quotes is literal data. Section header: '[' name ']' where name matches [A-Za-z0-9_]+, whitespace allowed just inside the brackets (`[ hub ]` is `hub`); anything but whitespace/comment after ']' is malformed. Keys match [A-Za-z0-9_-]+ (lane keys like `1-3` need the dash). Values are typed by shape, classified by the first whitespace-delimited token: `$D.CC` is money — a '$', digits, then EXACTLY two decimals ($12.5, $12, $1,200.00 are all bad); a token ending '%' is a percent — [0-9]+ with an optional 1-2 digit decimal part (12.5%, 4%, 0.25% ok; 12.345%, -4%, bare % bad), stored as basis points; `"..."` is a string — no escape sequences, must close on its own line; `true`/`false` are bools; an optional '-' plus digits is an integer (i64; no '+', no underscores). There is NO float type: bare `12.50` is not a value. After a complete value only whitespace or a comment may follow. Keys before any section header are an error; duplicate section names and duplicate keys within one section are errors (same key in different sections is fine).

Parse API: `RateFile::parse(&str) -> Result<RateFile, ParseError>`; `sections() -> Vec<&str>` and `keys(section) -> Vec<&str>` report DOCUMENT order (keys of an unknown section = empty vec); `get(section, key) -> Option<&Value>`. `Value` is an enum: Str(String), Int(i64), Money(Money), Percent(u32), Bool(bool) — Debug/Clone/PartialEq. `ParseError` has pub `line: usize` (1-based) and pub `message: String`, Display `rates:{line}: {message}`. The exact message strings are contract (audit greps our load logs): "key before any section", "malformed section header", "duplicate section '{name}'" (reported at the second header), "malformed key" (also for an empty key), "expected '=' after key", "unterminated string", "bad money amount", "bad percent", "unrecognized value" (unclassifiable or empty value), "trailing characters after value", "duplicate key '{key}' in section '{section}'".

TARIFF SCHEMA (src/tariff.rs, station/lane tables in src/zones.rs, scales in src/brackets.rs, surcharge defs in src/surcharge.rs). `Tariff::parse(&str)` runs the parser then validates; `Tariff::load(path)` reads a file first (tests load "tariffs/mainline.rates" relative to the crate root). Errors are `TariffError` (Debug + Display): a parse failure displays exactly like the ParseError ("rates:{line}: ..."); a schema failure displays "schema: {message}"; an unreadable file displays "io: {message}". Schema validation order is fixed: first required sections in order — missing [meta], then [zones], then [lanes] -> "missing section [meta]" etc. (zones/lanes may be empty but must exist); then walk sections in document order, and within a section walk keys in document order, reporting the first problem. Message shapes: "[{section}] {key}: {problem}" for a key problem, "[{section}]: {problem}" for a section problem. The tariff sections:
- [meta]: `name` (string, required). Anything else: "unknown key".
- [zones]: each key is a station code, exactly three chars A-Z ("bad station code" otherwise, e.g. `Alt`); each value an int 1..9 ("zone must be 1..9"; non-int: "expected int").
- [lanes]: each key `O-D` with O,D single digits 1-9 ("bad lane"); value is a string scale name S ("expected string") and section [scale_S] must exist: "unknown scale 'S'". Lanes are DIRECTIONAL: 1-2 does not imply 2-1.
- [scale_S] (S matches [A-Z0-9]+, else "bad scale name"): `minimum` (money, required) and bracket keys `from_N` (money) where N is the bracket floor in lbs — N must parse as a non-negative integer ("bad bracket key") and be a multiple of 100 ("floor must be a multiple of 100"); `from_0` is required. Other keys: "unknown key". Missing required keys report "[scale_S]: missing key 'minimum'" / "missing key 'from_0'" AFTER the per-key walk.
- [surcharge_C] (C matches [A-Z0-9]+ else "bad surcharge code"): `label` (string, required), `kind` (string, required, "percent" or "flat" — anything else: "must be 'percent' or 'flat'"), `amount` (required; a percent value when kind = percent else "expected percent"; money when kind = flat else "expected money"), `cap` (money, optional, legal on both kinds), `requires` (string, optional). Unknown keys rejected. Surcharge application order = the order the surcharge sections appear in the sheet.
- Any other section name: "unknown section".

RATING (src/brackets.rs + src/quote.rs). A `Shipment` is a plain pub-field struct: `reference: String`, `origin: String`, `dest: String`, `weight_lbs: u32`, `flags: BTreeSet<String>`, `waivers: BTreeSet<String>` (derive Debug/Clone). `quote(&Tariff, &Shipment) -> Result<Quote, QuoteError>` where `QuoteError` (Debug/PartialEq/Eq + Display) is: BadWeight — weight 0, display "weight must be at least 1 lb" (checked first); UnknownStation(String) — origin then dest, display "unknown station 'XXX'"; NoLane(u8, u8) — display "no lane from zone 2 to zone 1".
Linehaul math, in this exact order:
1. Billed hundredweight: cwt = weight rounded UP to the next full 100 lb, i.e. (w+99)/100. 101 lb bills as 2 cwt.
2. Bracket selection: the bracket with the LARGEST floor <= weight — floors are INCLUSIVE, so at exactly 500 lb a from_500 bracket applies, at 499 it does not. Natural linehaul = cwt * rate.
3. Deficit-weight check: for EVERY higher bracket (floor F > weight), price the shipment as if it weighed F (F/100 cwt at that bracket's rate). If the cheapest such alternative is STRICTLY cheaper than the natural linehaul, rebill at that floor (ties keep the natural bracket; if two alternatives tie each other, the lower floor wins). Worked on scale B of the shipped sheet (from_0 $22.50, from_500 $19.10, from_2000 $16.40, from_5000 $13.75, minimum $95.00): 1850 lb -> 19 cwt @ $19.10 = $362.90 natural; at 2000 lb: 20 cwt @ $16.40 = $328.00 (cheaper), at 5000: 50 @ $13.75 = $687.50 (not); so rebill at 2000 lb for $328.00. And 499 lb -> 5 cwt @ $22.50 = $112.50 natural, rebilled at 500 lb: 5 cwt @ $19.10 = $95.50. The check scans ALL higher brackets, not just the next one: with from_0 $30.00 / from_500 $28.00 / from_1000 $10.00, a 400 lb shipment (natural 4*$30 = $120.00, at 500: 5*$28 = $140.00, at 1000: 10*$10 = $100.00) rebills at 1000 lb.
4. Scale minimum, applied AFTER the deficit check: if the linehaul so far is below `minimum`, raise it to exactly `minimum`.
The result carries the full story in a `Linehaul` struct (pub fields; Debug/Clone): `weight_lbs: u32`, `cwt: u32` (natural billed cwt), `rate: Money` (natural bracket rate), `base: Money` (natural cost), `rebill: Option<Rebill>` with `Rebill { lbs: u32, cwt: u32, rate: Money, cost: Money }`, `minimum_applied: bool`, `total: Money` (after rebill + minimum).

SURCHARGE STACKING (src/surcharge.rs + src/quote.rs). Starting from linehaul total, walk the sheet's surcharges in order and keep a running total:
- If the surcharge has `requires` and the shipment's flags do not contain that string: SKIP it silently — it does not appear in the quote at all.
- Else if the surcharge's code is in the shipment's waivers: record it as waived, amount $0.00, running total unchanged (waivers are per-contract; the desk wants them visible in the audit trail).
- Else compute: percent kind -> `running.percent_of(bp)` (half-up, base is the CURRENT running total — order matters and the sheet order is the law); flat kind -> the sheet amount. If a `cap` is set and the computed amount exceeds it, charge exactly the cap and mark it capped. Add to the running total.
Each application is an `Applied` struct (pub fields; Debug/Clone): `code: String`, `percent: Option<u32>` (bp, None for flat), `base: Option<Money>` (the running total a percent was computed on, None for flat), `amount: Money`, `cap: Option<Money>` (copied from the definition), `capped: bool`, `waived: bool`. Worked: linehaul $100.00 with FUEL 10% then HAND flat $5.00 -> $110.00 -> $115.00; the same two reversed -> $105.00 -> 10% of $105.00 = $10.50 -> $115.50.
`Quote` (pub fields; Debug/Clone): `reference`, `origin`, `dest`, `weight_lbs`, `origin_zone: u8`, `dest_zone: u8`, `scale: String`, `linehaul: Linehaul`, `surcharges: Vec<Applied>`, `total: Money`.

AUDIT TRAIL (src/audit.rs). `audit::render(&Quote) -> String` — the exact plaintext the desk files; every line ends with '\n' (the last one too). Percent values render with the shortest exact decimal: 1250 bp -> "12.5", 400 -> "4", 25 -> "0.25". Line templates, in emission order:
  quote {reference} {origin} -> {dest} {weight} lb
  lane: zone {o} -> zone {d}, scale {S}
  linehaul: {cwt} cwt @ {rate}/cwt = {base} | total {base}
  deficit-weight: rebill at {lbs} lb, {cwt} cwt @ {rate}/cwt = {cost} | total {cost}     (only when rebilled)
  minimum-charge: raised to {min} | total {min}                                          (only when raised)
  surcharge {CODE} ({pct}% of {base}): +{amount} | total {running}                       (percent)
  surcharge {CODE} ({pct}% of {base}, capped at {cap}): +{amount} | total {running}      (percent, cap hit)
  surcharge {CODE} (flat): +{amount} | total {running}                                   (flat; cap hit: "(flat, capped at {cap})")
  surcharge {CODE}: waived by contract | total {running}                                 (waived)
  total: {total}
Two fully worked quotes against the shipped sheet (zones: ALT/BRK=1, COV=2, DRM/RVI=3, FLD=4; lane 1-3 -> scale B; surcharge order FUEL 12.5%, RESI flat $45.00 requires "residential", LIFT flat $62.50 requires "liftgate", PEAK 4% cap $25.00):
1) Q-1042, ALT -> RVI, 1850 lb, flags {residential}: natural 19 cwt @ $19.10 = $362.90; rebill at 2000 lb = $328.00; FUEL 12.5% of $328.00 = $41.00 -> $369.00; RESI +$45.00 -> $414.00; LIFT skipped (no flag); PEAK 4% of $414.00 = $16.56 -> $430.56. Render:
quote Q-1042 ALT -> RVI 1850 lb
lane: zone 1 -> zone 3, scale B
linehaul: 19 cwt @ $19.10/cwt = $362.90 | total $362.90
deficit-weight: rebill at 2000 lb, 20 cwt @ $16.40/cwt = $328.00 | total $328.00
surcharge FUEL (12.5% of $328.00): +$41.00 | total $369.00
surcharge RESI (flat): +$45.00 | total $414.00
surcharge PEAK (4% of $414.00): +$16.56 | total $430.56
total: $430.56
2) Q-2077, COV -> DRM, 6200 lb, flags {liftgate}, waivers {FUEL}: 62 cwt @ $13.75 = $852.50 (no higher bracket); FUEL waived; LIFT +$62.50 -> $915.00; PEAK 4% of $915.00 = $36.60, capped at $25.00 -> $940.00. Render:
quote Q-2077 COV -> DRM 6200 lb
lane: zone 2 -> zone 3, scale B
linehaul: 62 cwt @ $13.75/cwt = $852.50 | total $852.50
surcharge FUEL: waived by contract | total $852.50
surcharge LIFT (flat): +$62.50 | total $915.00
surcharge PEAK (4% of $915.00, capped at $25.00): +$25.00 | total $940.00
total: $940.00
And the minimum in action: ALT -> BRK (lane 1-1, scale A: minimum $89.00, from_0 $16.80) at 120 lb: 2 cwt @ $16.80 = $33.60, no cheaper rebill, raised to $89.00; FUEL 12.5% of $89.00 = $11.13 -> $100.13; PEAK 4% of $100.13 = $4.01 -> $104.14 — the audit shows "minimum-charge: raised to $89.00 | total $89.00" between the linehaul and FUEL lines.

Build it exactly to this spec — the suite checks the strings character for character, and finance signs off on the arithmetic, not on vibes.
